devanagari: सूत उवाच – । संस्तुतो भगवानित्थं मार्कण्डेयेन धीमता । नारायणो नरसखः प्रीत आह भृगूद्वहम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: sūta uvāca – saṃstuto bhagavānitthaṃ mārkaṇḍeyena dhīmatā nārāyaṇo narasakhaḥ prīta āha bhṛgūdvaham

translation:
Sūta continued:
When the glorious Lord Nārāyaṇa, the friend of (the sage) Nara, was eulogized in these words by the intelligent sage Mārkaṇḍeya, he became highly pleased and spoke to the descendant of the Bhṛgu race.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । भो भो ब्रह्मर्षिवर्योऽसि सिद्ध आत्मसमाधिना । मयि भक्त्यानपायिन्या तपःस्वाध्यायसंयमैः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca bho bho brahmarṣivaryo mayi bhaktyānapāyinyā tapaḥsvādhyāyasaṃyamaiḥ

translation:
The glorious Lord said:
O excellent Brāhmaṇa sage, you have attained perfection (Siddha-hood) by virtue of your concentration of minḍ, through unswerving devotion to me and by your austere penance, study of the Vedas and self-control.

devanagari: वयं ते परितुष्टाः स्म त्वद्बृहद्व्रतचर्यया । वरं प्रतीच्छ भद्रं ते वरदेशादभीप्सितम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: vayaṃ te parituṣṭāḥ sma tvadbṛhadvratacaryayā varaṃ pratīccha bhadraṃ te varadeśādabhīpsitam
translation: We (i.e. all gods) are highly pleased with you by your observance of the great bow (of life-long celibacy). May you be happy and prosperous! Accept any desired boon from me who am the Supreme Ruler of those capable of conferring boons.

devanagari: श्रीऋषिरुवाच । जितं ते देवदेवेश प्रपन्नार्तिहराच्युत । वरेणैतावतालं नो यद् भवान् समदृश्यत ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīṛṣiruvāca jitaṃ te devadeveśa prapannārtiharācyuta vareṇaitāvatālaṃ no yad bhavān samadṛśyata

translation:
The sage (Mārkaṇḍeya) replied:
Victory to you. O Ruler of gods of celestials (like Brahmā, Rudra) You are the remover of the agonies of those who earnestly resort to you. O Immutable Lord, that your Lordship have been gracious enough to reveal yourself to me is in itself the greatest boon bestowed on me. And that is quite enough for me.

devanagari: गृहीत्वाजादयो यस्य श्रीमत् पादाब्जदर्शनम् । मनसा योगपक्वेन स भवान् मेऽक्षगोचरः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhītvājādayo yasya śrīmat pādābjadarśanam manasā yogapakvena sa bhavān me

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental single from √ pac class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ pac class 4 verb]

translation: Even great gods like Brahmā regard themselves as having achieved their objective in life when they get in their minds purified (and ‘ripened’) through Yoga, a (mental) vision of your glorious Lotus feet. That Supreme Lord has revealed himself within the range of my perception (before my very eyes).

devanagari: अथाप्यम्बुजपत्राक्ष पुण्यश्लोकशिखामणे । द्रक्ष्ये मायां यया लोकः सपालो वेद सद्भिदाम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: athāpyambujapatrākṣa puṇyaślokaśikhāmaṇe drakṣye māyāṃ yayā lokaḥ sapālo veda sadbhidām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: O Lord with eyes beautiful like lotus-petals, I would, however, like to have a vision of your Māyā, deluded by whom the world along with the Protectors of the spheres like Brahmā, experience differences (such as gods, Subhuman beings, human beings, etc.) when you are the only Reality[1] (without a second and there is no basis for that experience).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Padaratnāvalī: How Sat i.e. Brahman or yourself is quite distinct and diffe­rent from all other things.

devanagari: सूत उवाच । इतीडितोऽर्चितः कामम् ऋषिणा भगवान् मुने । तथेति स स्मयन् प्रागाद् बदर्याश्रममीश्वरः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: sūta uvāca itīḍito tatheti sa smayan prāgād badaryāśramamīśvaraḥ

translation:
Sūta said:
Thus devoutly praised and worshipped by the sage (Mārkaṇḍeya), the Almighty Lord assured, “Yes, you will see it”, and smilingly returned to his hermitage at Badarī, O sage Śaunaka.

devanagari: तमेव चिन्तयन्नर्थम् ऋषिः स्वाश्रम एव सः । वसन् अग्न्यर्कसोमाम्बु भूवायुवियदात्मसु ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tameva cintayannartham ṛṣiḥ svāśrama eva saḥ vasan agnyarkasomāmbu bhūvāyuviyadātmasu

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ vas class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vas class 1 verb]

translation: Mārkaṇḍeya continued to stay in his hermitage brooding over the promised vision (of Māyā). While contemplating he saw Hari revealed in the sacrificial fire, the sun, the moon, waters, the earth, the wind, the sky, in his own self and everywhere around him. He worshipped him with articles of worship conceived mentally. Sometimes he forgot to worship when he was overwhelmed and lost in the flood of loving devotion.

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां द्वादशस्कन्धे मायादर्शनं नाम नवमोऽध्यायः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ dvādaśaskandhe māyādarśanaṃ nāma navamo
translation: Mārkaṇḍeya continued to stay in his hermitage brooding over the promised vision (of Māyā). While contemplating he saw Hari revealed in the sacrificial fire, the sun, the moon, waters, the earth, the wind, the sky, in his own self and everywhere around him. He worshipped him with articles of worship conceived mentally. Sometimes he forgot to worship when he was overwhelmed and lost in the flood of loving devotion.

devanagari: तस्यैकदा भृगुश्रेष्ठ पुष्पभद्रातटे मुनेः । उपासीनस्य सन्ध्यायां ब्रह्मन् वायुरभून्महान् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tasyaikadā bhṛguśreṣṭha puṣpabhadrātaṭe muneḥ upāsīnasya sandhyāyāṃ brahman vāyurabhūnmahān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single], [imperative passive first single]

translation: O pre-eminent member of the Bhṛgu clan I On one occasion, while he was engaged in religious meditation in the evening on the bank of the Puṣpabhaḍrā, tempestuous wind suddenly began to blow.

devanagari: तं चण्डशब्दं समुदीरयन्तं । बलाहका अन्वभवन् करालाः । अक्षस्थविष्ठा मुमुचुस्तडिद्भिः । स्वनन्त उच्चैरभिवर्षधाराः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ caṇḍaśabdaṃ samudīrayantaṃ balāhakā anvabhavan karālāḥ akṣasthaviṣṭhā mumucustaḍidbhiḥ svananta uccairabhivarṣadhārāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]
[nominative plural from √ svan class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ svan class 1 verb]

translation: Following the furiously roaring blasts of the winds, terrific clouds overcast the sky. Attended with flashes of lightning and deafening thunders, they poured volleys of rain as big as the axles of chariots.

devanagari: ततो व्यदृश्यन्त चतुःसमुद्राः । समन्ततः क्ष्मातलमाग्रसन्तः । समीरवेगोर्मिभिरुग्रनक्र । महाभयावर्तगभीरघोषाः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tato vyadṛśyanta catuḥsamudrāḥ samantataḥ kṣmātalamāgrasantaḥ samīravegormibhirugranakra mahābhayāvartagabhīraghoṣāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ gras class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ gras class 1 verb]

translation: Then, following the torrential downpour, were seen oceans in all the four directions around, surging forward, overflooding and swallowing up the earth, while roaring loudly with (mountain-like) high waves tossed up by stormy winds and with terrible looking alligators and awefully [awfully?] big whirlpools.

devanagari: अन्तर्बहिश्चाद्भिरतिद्युभिः खरैः । शतह्रदाभिरुपतापितं जगत् । चतुर्विधं वीक्ष्य सहात्मना मुनिः । जलाप्लुतां क्ष्मां विमनाः समत्रसत् ॥ १३...
english_devnagari: antarbahiścādbhiratidyubhiḥ kharaiḥ śatahradābhirupatāpitaṃ jagat caturvidhaṃ vīkṣya sahātmanā muniḥ jalāplutāṃ kṣmāṃ vimanāḥ samatrasat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sam class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sam class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sam class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ ras class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ ras class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ras class 1 verb]

translation: Seeing that the fourfold world of creatures (like mammals, plant-world, oviparous beings) along with himself are afflicted both inside and outside with waters that rose sky-high, with the scorching rays of the sun, blasts of the stormy winds and strokes of lightning and the whole earth is submerged in waters, the sage was deeply disturbed and afraid at heart.

devanagari: तस्यैवमुद्वीक्षत ऊर्मिभीषणः । प्रभञ्जनाघूर्णितवार्महार्णवः । आपूर्यमाणो वरषद्भिरम्बुदैः । क्ष्मामप्यधाद् द्वीपवर्षाद्रिभिः समम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyaivamudvīkṣata ūrmibhīṣaṇaḥ prabhañjanāghūrṇitavārmahārṇavaḥ āpūryamāṇo varaṣadbhirambudaiḥ kṣmāmapyadhād dvīpavarṣādribhiḥ samam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: Even as he was looking on (in consternation), the boundless ocean terrific-looking on account of (mountainous) waves, and with its waters tossed and whirling by tempestuous winds was filled by the pouring clouds, overflooded the earth and submerged it along with its continents, sub-continents and mountain-chains.

devanagari: सक्ष्मान्तरिक्षं सदिवं सभागणं । त्रैलोक्यमासीत् सह दिग्भिराप्लुतम् । स एक एवोर्वरितो महामुनिः । बभ्राम विक्षिप्य जटा जडान्धवत् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: sakṣmāntarikṣaṃ sadivaṃ sabhāgaṇaṃ trailokyamāsīt saha digbhirāplutam sa eka evorvarito mahāmuniḥ babhrāma vikṣipya jaṭā jaḍāndhavat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: All the worlds including the earth, the sky, the celestial world, and the stellar and planetary systems along with their inhabitants, and all the cardinal points were sub-merged and lost in that deluge. The sage Mārkaṇḍeya alone survived. He was drifting with his matted hairs scattered and hanging like a mad, blind, person.

devanagari: क्षुत्तृट्परीतो मकरैस्तिमिङ्गिलैः । उपद्रुतो वीचिनभस्वताहतः । तमस्यपारे पतितो भ्रमन् दिशो । न वेद खं गां च परिश्रमेषितः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣuttṛṭparīto makaraistimiṅgilaiḥ upadruto vīcinabhasvatāhataḥ tamasyapāre patito bhraman diśo na veda khaṃ gāṃ ca pariśrameṣitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ iṣ ]
[nominative single from √ iṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ iṣ class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ iṣ ]

translation: Sorely oppressed with hunger and thirst, troubled by crocodiles and whales, tossed and buffeted by stormy winds and waves, enveloped in endless darkness and overcome with fatigue, the sage drifted along and could not know the directions or the sky or the earth.

devanagari: क्वचिद् गतो महावर्ते तरलैस्ताडितः क्वचित् । यादोभिर्भक्ष्यते क्वापि स्वयं अन्योन्यघातिभिः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacid gato mahāvarte taralaistāḍitaḥ kvacit yādobhirbhakṣyate kvāpi svayaṃ anyonyaghātibhiḥ
translation: Sometimes he fell into a great whirl-pool; sometimes he was tossed at random by waves: at times he was bitten by acquatic animals who fought with each other while trying to catch hold of him.

devanagari: क्वचिच्छोकं क्वचिन्मोहं क्वचिद् दुखं सुखं भयम् । क्वचित् मृत्युमवाप्नोति व्याध्यादिभिरुतार्दितः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacicchokaṃ kvacinmohaṃ kvacid dukhaṃ sukhaṃ bhayam kvacit mṛtyumavāpnoti vyādhyādibhirutārditaḥ
translation: Sometimes he was over-come with grief, sometimes with infatuation: sometimes he experienced pleasure, sometimes pain and at times fear. Sometimes he felt that he was about to die, while at times, he suffered from diseases and other troubles.

devanagari: अयुतायतवर्षाणां सहस्राणि शतानि च । व्यतीयुर्भ्रमतः तस्मिन् विष्णुमायावृतात्मनः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: ayutāyatavarṣāṇāṃ sahasrāṇi śatāni ca vyatīyurbhramataḥ tasmin viṣṇumāyāvṛtātmanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active third plural], [perfect active third plural]

translation: Innumerable (billions upon billions) years passed away (he felt) while he was being drifted and tossed in that deluge and his self (mind) was hypnotised by the Māyā of Viṣṇu.

devanagari: स कदाचिद् भ्रमन् तस्मिन् पृथिव्याः ककुदि द्विजः । न्याग्रोधपोतं ददृशे फलपल्लवशोभितम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: sa kadācid bhraman tasmin pṛthivyāḥ kakudi dvijaḥ nyāgrodhapotaṃ dadṛśe phalapallavaśobhitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ śubh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ śubh class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ śubh ]

[nominative single from √ śubh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ śubh class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ śubh class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ śubh class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ śubh ], [accusative single from √ śubh ]

translation: While being tossed about at random (and thus, wandering) in the waters of the deluge, he caught sight of a young banyan tree adorned with fruit and foliage on a summit of the earth (protruding over waters).

devanagari: प्रागुत्तरस्यां शाखायां तस्यापि ददृशे शिशुम् । शयानं पर्णपुटके ग्रसन्तं प्रभया तमः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: prāguttarasyāṃ śākhāyāṃ tasyāpi dadṛśe śiśum śayānaṃ parṇapuṭake grasantaṃ prabhayā tamaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śī class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ śī class 2 verb]

translation: On the north-eastern branch, of that tree and on (the cavity of) its leaf, he beheld an infant (as if) swallowing up the darkness by its brilliant splendour.

devanagari: महामरकतश्यामं श्रीमद्वदनपङ्कजम् । कम्बुग्रीवं महोरस्कं सुनासं सुन्दरभ्रुवम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: mahāmarakataśyāmaṃ śrīmadvadanapaṅkajam kambugrīvaṃ mahoraskaṃ sunāsaṃ sundarabhruvam
translation: It was like a dark-emerald in complexion; its countenance was beautiful like lotus; it had a conch-shaped neck, broad chest, shapely nose and charming brows. It appeared graceful with its locks of curly hair moving to and fro by (the wind of) its breath; its beautiful ears like (the open side of) a conch, were adorned with pomegranate-like red flowers (or ear-rings of that shape and colour); its nectar-like white (sweet) smile was rendered rosy by the reddish splendour of its coral-like crimson lips.

devanagari: श्वासैजदलकाभातं कम्बुश्रीकर्णदाडिमम् । विद्रुमाधरभासेषत् शोणायित सुधास्मितम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: śvāsaijadalakābhātaṃ kambuśrīkarṇadāḍimam vidrumādharabhāseṣat śoṇāyita sudhāsmitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: It was like a dark-emerald in complexion; its countenance was beautiful like lotus; it had a conch-shaped neck, broad chest, shapely nose and charming brows. It appeared graceful with its locks of curly hair moving to and fro by (the wind of) its breath; its beautiful ears like (the open side of) a conch, were adorned with pomegranate-like red flowers (or ear-rings of that shape and colour); its nectar-like white (sweet) smile was rendered rosy by the reddish splendour of its coral-like crimson lips.

devanagari: पद्मगर्भारुणापाङ्गं हृद्यहासावलोकनम् । श्वासैजद्बलिसंविग्ननिम्ननाभिदलोदरम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: padmagarbhāruṇāpāṅgaṃ hṛdyahāsāvalokanam śvāsaijadbalisaṃvignanimnanābhidalodaram
translation: The ends of its eyes were reddish like the interior of a lotus; its smiling looks and glances captivated one’s heart; its belly, shaped like a leaf of a sacred fig tree, appeared beautiful with its deep navel covered with the throbbing folds of the belly which heaved up and down with its breaths.

devanagari: चार्वङ्गुलिभ्यां पाणिभ्याम् उन्नीय चरणाम्बुजम् । मुखे निधाय विप्रेन्द्रो धयन्तं वीक्ष्य विस्मितः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: cārvaṅgulibhyāṃ pāṇibhyām unnīya caraṇāmbujam mukhe nidhāya viprendro dhayantaṃ vīkṣya vismitaḥ
translation: The babe caught hold of its own lotus-foot with its hands of charming fingers, and placing it in its mouth, was sucking it.

devanagari: तद्दर्शनाद् वीतपरिश्रमो मुदा । प्रोत्फुल्लहृत्पद्मविलोचनाम्बुजः । प्रहृष्टरोमाद्भुतभावशङ्कितः । प्रष्टुं पुरस्तं प्रससार बालकम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: taddarśanād vītapariśramo mudā protphullahṛtpadmavilocanāmbujaḥ prahṛṣṭaromādbhutabhāvaśaṅkitaḥ praṣṭuṃ purastaṃ prasasāra bālakam
translation: The very sight of that infant, all his fatigue was removed and he felt refreshed. The lotuses in the form of his heart and eyes as if blossomed with ecstasy; (through the thrill of joy) his hairs stood on their ends. Though he was overcome with awe to see that miraculous form (the wonderful baby), he (made bold and) approached the babe to make enquiries of it.

devanagari: तावच्छिशोर्वै श्वसितेन भार्गवः । सोऽन्तः शरीरं मशको यथाविशत् । तत्राप्यदो न्यस्तमचष्ट कृत्स्नशो । यथा पुरामुह्यदतीव विस्मितः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tāvacchiśorvai śvasitena bhārgavaḥ so tatrāpyado nyastamacaṣṭa kṛtsnaśo yathā purāmuhyadatīva vismitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]
[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: In the meanwhile, along with the inhaling of the baby, Mārkaṇḍeya, the descendant of Bhṛgu was drawn inside (the baby’s) body like a mosquito. There (in the belly of the baby) he saw the entire universe arranged systematically as it was before the deluge—at which he was extremely surprised and confused.

devanagari: खं रोदसी भागणानद्रिसागरान् । द्वीपान् सवर्षान् ककुभः सुरासुरान् । वनानि देशान् सरितः पुराकरान् । खेटान् व्रजानाश्रमवर्णवृत्तयः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: khaṃ rodasī bhāgaṇānadrisāgarān dvīpān savarṣān kakubhaḥ surāsurān vanāni deśān saritaḥ purākarān kheṭān vrajānāśramavarṇavṛttayaḥ
translation: With wonderment he saw therein the sky, the earth, the aerial region, hosts of heavenly luminaries, mountains and oceans, continents along with their sub-continents, the cardinal points, gods and Asuras, forests, countries, rivers, cities, quarries or mines, villages, pastoral habitations, hermitages (or stages in life-āśrama), (Varṇas) of people engaged in their own. respective duties.

devanagari: महान्ति भूतान्यथ भौतिकान्यसौ । कालं च नानायुग कल्पकल्पनम् । यत्किञ्चिदन्यद् व्यवहारकारणं । ददर्श विश्वं सदिवावभासितम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: mahānti bhūtānyatha bhautikānyasau kālaṃ ca nānāyuga kalpakalpanam yatkiñcidanyad vyavahārakāraṇaṃ dadarśa viśvaṃ sadivāvabhāsitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ bhās ], [accusative single from √ bhās ]

translation: He further beheld there the five gross-elements (bhūtas) and their products, the Time with its various divisions such as yugas, kalpas and everything else that can act as the cause of the world—in short the whole universe was manifested here, as if it were a reality.

devanagari: हिमालयं पुष्पवहां च तां नदीं । निजाश्रमं तत्र ऋषीन् अपश्यत । विश्वं विपश्यञ्छ्वसिताच्छिशोर्वै । बहिर्निरस्तो न्यपतल्लयाब्धौ ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: himālayaṃ puṣpavahāṃ ca tāṃ nadīṃ nijāśramaṃ tatra ṛṣīn apaśyata viśvaṃ vipaśyañchvasitācchiśorvai bahirnirasto nyapatallayābdhau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: He also espied the mountain Himalaya, that very same river Puṣpavahā and his own hermitage on its bank and sages dwelling in his neighbourhood. Even as he was keenly observing that universe, he was exhaled out of the body of the child and fell in the sea of deluge.

devanagari: तस्मिन् पृथिव्याः ककुदि प्ररूढं । वटं च तत्पर्णपुटे शयानम् । तोकं च तत्प्रेमसुधास्मितेन । निरीक्षितोऽपाङ्गनिरीक्षणेन ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmin pṛthivyāḥ kakudi prarūḍhaṃ vaṭaṃ ca tatparṇapuṭe śayānam tokaṃ ca tatpremasudhāsmitena nirīkṣito

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śī class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ śī class 2 verb]

translation: He perceived once more the banyan tree growing on the summit of the (submerged) earth and the child that was lying on the hollow leaf and that he was being observed with, its side-long glances accompanied with nectar like sweet smiles (by that mysterious child).

devanagari: अथ तं बालकं वीक्ष्य नेत्राभ्यां धिष्ठितं हृदि । अभ्ययादतिसङ्क्लिष्टः परिष्वक्तुं अधोक्षजम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: atha taṃ bālakaṃ vīkṣya netrābhyāṃ dhiṣṭhitaṃ hṛdi abhyayādatisaṅkliṣṭaḥ pariṣvaktuṃ adhokṣajam
translation: Then Mārkaṇḍeya who was over-exhausted perceived that child who, entering through his eyes, had enthroned himself firmly in his heart, proceeded to embrace it who was (no other than) the Supra-sensuous Lord Viṣṇu!

devanagari: तावत्स भगवान् साक्षात् योगाधीशो गुहाशयः । अन्तर्दधे ऋषेः सद्यो यथेहानीशनिर्मिता ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tāvatsa bhagavān sākṣāt yogādhīśo guhāśayaḥ antardadhe ṛṣeḥ sadyo yathehānīśanirmitā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ mā class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ mā class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ mā class 4 verb]

translation: But that very moment, the infant who was the glorious Lord Viṣṇu himself, the absolute master of Yoga who abides in the hearts of all instantly vanished from the sage like the desire (or undertaking) of an ungodly (or an unlucky) person.

devanagari: तमन्वथ वटो ब्रह्मन् सलिलं लोकसम्प्लवः । तिरोधायि क्षणादस्य स्वाश्रमे पूर्ववन् स्थितः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: tamanvatha vaṭo brahman salilaṃ lokasamplavaḥ tirodhāyi kṣaṇādasya svāśrame pūrvavan sthitaḥ
translation:
O Brāhmaṇa sage! Following the Lord, the banyan tree, the cosmic waters that deluged the whole world (and submerged it) disappeared in a moment and the sage found himself in his own hermitage as before.
